Two AIs just became a team and that team can build an entire business. Most people are using AI completely wrong. They open one tool, they type one prompt, they get one answer and they think that's the whole game. But the people getting real results right now. They're doing something completely different. They're running AI like a company. One AI thinks, one AI builds. Together they create a system that never stops working. GLM5 Turbo is the builder brain. Claude Opus is the strategist brain. And when you combine them, something genuinely insane happens. This isn't about better prompts. This is about AI teams. Stick around because this is one of those videos that changes how you work forever. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. And Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you drop one below before you leave. Here's the thing most people get wrong about AI. They treat it like a search engine. They open one tool, type a question, get an answer, and move on. And that works fine for simple stuff, but it is not a system. It's not a workflow. And it's definitely not a team. The people who are getting serious traction right now are not just using better prompts. They are using multiple AIs in a coordinated way. Each one doing a specific job, each one feeding directly into the next. And when you combine GLM5 Turbo with Claude Opus, you don't just use AI anymore. You run AI. Today, I'm going to break down exactly how this works across four completely different use cases. Entrepreneurs, students, teachers, and marketing teams. By the end of this video, you are going to have a complete workflow you can actually start using today. So, let's get into it. GLM5 Turbo is the builder. It is a 744 billion parameter openweight model built specifically for coding system engineering and automation logic. If you need something made a tool, a backend, a script, a full automation workflow, GLM5 makes it. It is fast, technical and precise. Think of it as a developer on your team who never sleeps, never gets tired, and never asks for a deadline extension. Claude Opopus is the strategist. It is built for complex reasoning, deep document analysis, and multi-step planning across enormous amounts of information. It can handle up to a million tokens at once. That means you can hand it an entire business worth of research, competitor analysis, customer feedback, and strategy documents. And it will actually read all of it, understand all of it, and turn all of it into something you can act on. When you combine them, the workflow becomes this. Claude Opus thinks GLM5 builds, Claude Opus reviews, GLM5 executes. That is a closed production loop strategy, build, review, automate. And once you set this up properly, it runs like a machine. You stop working inside the business and start running the business. Real quick, if you want to learn how to build AI systems exactly like this one, real workflows, automations, and AI team setups that actually save you hours every single week, go check out the AI profit boardroom. It is where entrepreneurs are learning to build smarter AI systems and get more done without adding more to their plate. The link is in the description. Go check it out and come back. Now, let's keep going. Let's say you are building a new offer, a product or a service completely from scratch. Here is exactly how the 2 AI workflow runs from start to finish. You start with Claude Opus. You give it your market, your competitors, your rough idea. You say something like, "Analyze this space, identify the biggest gap, and give me a positioning strategy I can move on." right now. Claude thinks deeply. It gives you a clear business concept, a defined target audience, and a direction that actually makes sense. No guesswork, no weeks of research, a clear strategic output in minutes that you can take straight into the next step. Then you take that output to GLM5. You say, "Build this, write the backend, generate the landing page code, create the automation logic. GLM5 builds it clean, functional, and ready to deploy without you having to touch a single line of code yourself. Then back to Claude Opus, write the sales page, the email funnel, the offer positioning. Claude handles all of the long form reasoning and it keeps everything completely consistent because it remembers the full strategy context you gave it right at the beginning. Then back to GLM5 for the final layer. CRM scripts, website backend, automation workflows, AI chatbot for handling inquiries, everything connected, everything working together. That is a complete business system built by two AIs working as a coordinated team. What used to take a full agency now takes a workflow. This one is for anyone in school, university, or doing any kind of serious researchbased work. You upload your research papers, your lecture slides, and your textbook chapters directly into Claude Opus. Then you say, "Summarize everything, give me the key insights across all of it, identify the research gaps, and help me find a strong angle for my project." Claude reads every single document. Because its context window is enormous, it actually processes the whole thing rather than skimming it. And it gives you a structured summary with real insights that would have taken you two full weeks to pull together manually. Then you take those research gaps straight to GLM5. You ask it to build a simulation, generate a clean data set or create a working model to test your hypothesis. GLM5 builds the technical tools. You bring the thinking and the judgment. Then Claude writes the final paper clean, properly structured with citations and references all handled. That is an AI research assistant working around the clock for you without taking any of the credit. Teachers carry one of the heaviest admin loads of any profession out there. Lesson plans, assignments, grading, feedback, curriculum mapping. It never stops. This workflow changes that completely. Claude Opus analyses your curriculum. You give it your learning objectives and it generates a full lecture plan that actually aligns with what you need to cover. It creates assignments built around your specific outcomes. You give it the input and it does the structure. Then GLM5 builds the interactive tools your students actually want to use. Quizzes, coding exercises, visual simulations, custom learning apps, all of it built to your specification and ready to drop straight into your classroom without any technical knowledge on your part. Then back to Claude for the assessment layer. It grades assignments consistently, identifies patterns in how your students are responding, and generates personalized feedback for every single student. What used to take your entire weekend now takes a single afternoon. And the feedback your students get is more detailed and more useful than most of them have ever received before. Here is the big idea. The future of AI is not one model doing everything. It is multiple AI systems working together like a company. Each one playing to its strengths. Each one feeding directly into the next. GLM5 is the engineer. Claude Opus is the strategist. Together they are not just a stack of tools. They are a system. And the people who learn how to orchestrate that system right now are going to be operating at a level most people have not even started thinking about yet. This is not a future thing.
